issues.claim: Humans have only about twenty thousand genes — fewer than a grain of rice.
issues.problem: True but the comparison is a popular-science talking point rather than an AP-exam fact. Rice (Oryza sativa) is typically cited at ~35,000-50,000 protein-coding genes, so the claim holds, but students sometimes mis-remember this as 'rice has more DNA' (genome size) which is a separate idea. Not wrong, just rhetorical.
issues.suggested_fix: Optional: clarify 'fewer protein-coding genes than a rice plant' to head off the genome-size confusion. No change required for accuracy.

issues.claim: Human cells make over a hundred thousand different proteins.
issues.problem: Proteome size estimates vary widely in the literature (often cited 80k-400k+ isoforms via alternative splicing and PTMs). 'Over a hundred thousand' is a defensible round number used in many AP-aligned textbooks, so acceptable, but not a hard exam fact.
issues.suggested_fix: Optionally soften to 'many tens of thousands of distinct protein isoforms' to avoid implying a precise figure.

issues.claim: The TATA box about twenty-five base pairs upstream of the gene.
issues.problem: Slightly imprecise — the TATA box is typically ~25-35 bp upstream of the transcription start site (often quoted as the -25 to -30 region). The number cited is within the accepted range, so this is acceptable shorthand.
issues.suggested_fix: Optional: 'roughly 25 to 30 base pairs upstream.' Not required.

overall_assessment: Script is factually accurate and AP-rigor appropriate. Central dogma, transcription directionality, mRNA processing, ribosome subunit sizes (80S/70S, A/P/E), codon table facts (64/61/3, AUG=Met, UAA/UAG/UGA), ribozyme catalysis of the peptide bond, lac/trp operon logic, and the sickle-cell missense example (GAG to GTG, glu to val at beta-globin position 6) are all correctly stated. The worked pause problem (template 3'-TACGGCATT-5' yielding mRNA 5'-AUGCCGUAA-3', Met-Pro-stop) checks out. No hallucinations or critical errors found; only minor rhetorical flourishes worth softening.
